---
title: "Hi, I'm Gonzalo"
about:
  template: jolla
  id: about-block
  image: images/profile.jpg
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 18em
  links:
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:gonzalo.llamosas@uma.es
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/gonzalo-llamosas-garcia/
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/lgg18106
    - icon: twitter-x
      text: X
      href: https://x.com/gonzalollamosas
    - text: Scholar
      href: https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=O8gTD4AAAAAJ&hl=en
    - text: ORCID
      href: https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4407-2845
---

::: {#about-block}
:::

I am **Assistant Professor in Public Finance** at the [University of Málaga](https://www.uma.es), accredited by ANECA as Associate Professor.

My research sits at the intersection of **fiscal policy**, **behavioural economics** and **data**, with growing attention to how artificial intelligence is reshaping the tax base of advanced economies. I currently work on the effects of fiscal consolidation on public debt sustainability in the EU-27, on the design of financial-education policies, and on how human decisions interact with fiscal-policy outcomes.

My recent work has appeared in the *Journal of Behavioral and Experimental Economics* (2025) and *Global Policy* (2020). Outside academia, I host [**Outlook Project**](outlook-project.qmd), a YouTube channel on economics, data and human decisions.

## Recent

- **April 2026** · Working paper *"Does Fiscal Consolidation Reduce Public Debt? Evidence from the EU-27"* submitted to *[journal]*.
- **2025** · Published *"What Works in Financial Education?"* in [*Journal of Behavioral and Experimental Economics*](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.socec.2025.102401) (Q1).
- **Coming soon** · Launching the [Outlook Project](outlook-project.qmd) YouTube channel on economics, data and human decisions.

## En español

> *Soy profesor ayudante doctor en Hacienda Pública en la Universidad de Málaga, acreditado por ANECA como profesor contratado doctor. Mi investigación combina política fiscal, economía conductual y datos, con atención creciente al impacto de la inteligencia artificial sobre la base fiscal de las economías avanzadas.*
